title: update ginkgo to v2
body:
I plan on using some new features for benchmarking Podman.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM
Unit tests all failing

> Unit tests all failing

Working now. Looks like a regression in the --output-dir flag in ginkgo which I worked around.
The latest commits cut the execution in half on my machine. Let's see how CI (dis)likes them.
OK, it seems like ginkgo hangs at the very end of the tests.  Needs further investigation :*(
I guess we're hitting https://onsi.github.io/ginkgo/#some-subtle-parallel-testing-gotchas
What a mess ... for some reason `os.Getuid()` returns 0 in the tests when run as a rootless user

> What a mess ... for some reason `os.Getuid()` returns 0 in the tests when run as a rootless user

I guess we import something in the tests that causes us to join the podman user namespace (maybe pkg/rootless?)
Yes you are in the user namespace.

I am staring at this for hours.

It worked before, it works when using `go test ...` but it does not work with `ginkgo` v2. I do not yet understand _why_ that would lead to joining the user NS.
Note: It's not happening in a minimal example using ginko v2.
My only theory would be that this runs https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/8b6f911e4818d02bca65ff263dcb61bfe082a470/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c#L282
@giuseppe Any ideas?

I think we need something like:

```diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index af9a2b7f6..74e0a4eed 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -56,7 +56,9 @@ BUILDTAGS ?= \
        $(shell hack/systemd_tag.sh) \
        $(shell hack/libsubid_tag.sh) \
        exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper \
-       seccomp
+       seccomp \
+       native_rootless
+
 PYTHON ?= $(shell command -v python3 python|head -n1)
 PKG_MANAGER ?= $(shell command -v dnf yum|head -n1)
 # ~/.local/bin is not in PATH on all systems
diff --git a/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c b/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c
index 94bd40f86..2471c0a89 100644
--- a/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c
+++ b/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c
@@ -215,7 +215,7 @@ can_use_shortcut ()
   bool ret = true;
   int argc;
 
-#ifdef DISABLE_JOIN_SHORTCUT
+#ifndef ENABLE_JOIN_SHORTCUT
   return false;
 #endif
 
diff --git a/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.go b/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.go
index cff6de5a3..d933fee42 100644
--- a/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.go
+++ b/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.go
@@ -29,7 +29,7 @@ import (
 )
 
 /*
-#cgo remoteclient CFLAGS: -Wall -Werror -DDISABLE_JOIN_SHORTCUT
+#cgo native_rootless CFLAGS: -Wall -Werror -DENABLE_JOIN_SHORTCUT
 #include <stdlib.h>
 #include <sys/types.h>
 extern uid_t rootless_uid();
```
that fixed the problem with ginkgo2 I was having as well.

> that fixed the problem with ginkgo2 I was having as well.

Same here for the user NS fart.  I still don't understand why ginkgo v2 would trigger that code path but not `go test` or ginkgo v1 but I am happy enough it's working now.

Let's see if that also fixes the dangling processes keeping ginkgo from finishing the reports (and hence triggering the time outs)>

> Let's see if that also fixes the dangling processes keeping ginkgo from finishing the reports (and hence triggering the time outs)

Still seeing it locally :(

Not much progress:
* It hangs with `ginkgo` v2
* It does not hang with v1
* It only hangs when run in parallel and apparently rootless seems to hang reproducibly in CI and locally
* It's very likely related to https://onsi.github.io/ginkgo/#some-subtle-parallel-testing-gotchas but I fear that we are hitting this "corner cases" very hard

I may use the benchmarking features of v1 for now but I am afraid we need to move to v2 at some point.
Talked with @baude and we agreed to abandon this PR for now. ginkgo v1 supports benchmarking as well and updating to v2 is not possible at the time.
